title: 10 best herbal essential oils for puffy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for puffy eyes are plant-derived extracts used to alleviate puffiness and discomfort around the eyes. These oils help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin. The best herbal essential oils for puffy eyes include Aloe barbadensis, Cucumis sativus, Calendula officinalis, Hamamelis virginiana, Vitis vinifera, Euphorbia trigona, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, puffy eyes can cause fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and reduced productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as aloe vera gel and vitamins A, C, and E, work together to reduce puffiness and dark circles. When applied topically, the oil cools and calms the skin around the eyes, reducing swelling and discoloration. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye puffiness, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, soothing and calming the skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in herbal aloe essential oil neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.

Cooling and Soothing: Herbal aloe essential oil has a cooling effect when applied topically, which helps to reduce redness, irritation, and puffiness, while also providing long-lasting hydration and relief to the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help reduce inflammation and ease puffiness.
Leaves: Abundant in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, making it an excellent source of antioxidants for soothing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and rinse them under cool running water for 10 minutes.
Blend 2 cups of aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup of coconut oil and heat the mixture to 180°f over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the essential oil to cool and settle at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and limonene, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure in the delicate skin around the eyes. This natural relief can help to depuff and refresh the appearance of the eye area, leaving it looking smoother and more radiant. Regular use of Tasmanian blue gum essential oils may also help to promote healthy circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains compounds like caryophyllene and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area.

Antioxidant properties: The oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, promoting a more radiant and rested appearance.

Decongestant and vasoconstrictor properties: The essential oil's decongestant properties can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffiness, while its vasoconstrictor properties can help constrict blood vessels and improve circulation to reduce dark circles and eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe puffy eyes.
Barks: The barks contain tannins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning when they are most aromatic.
Dry the leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500 milliliters of distilled water for 1 hour.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its natural cooling and decongesting properties. When applied topically, peppermint oil can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes, providing instant relief from puffiness. Its refreshing and invigorating aroma also helps to awaken and brighten the entire eye area, leaving you with a more radiant and rested appearance. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, further reducing the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil helps to reduce puffiness by reducing inflammation and swelling around the eye area, providing relief from discomfort and tension.

Cooling and calming effects: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil provides a cooling sensation when applied topically, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.

Decongestant properties: Peppermint essential oil's decongestant properties help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure, which are common causes of puffy eyes, by thinning mucus and promoting its drainage, thus alleviating puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce puffiness and ease tension in the skin.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain high levels of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that can help to alleviate puffy eyes by reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves at dawn when the oil content is highest and freshness assured.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours or until crumbly texture achieved.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 500 ml of water at atmospheric pressure for 1 hour.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol through decantation and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Bottle the mentha x piperita essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically, it constricts blood vessels and reduces swelling, alleviating the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, lavender oil's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and tension, which are common contributors to eye puffiness. As a result, English lavender essential oils can provide fast and effective relief from puffy eyes, leaving you with a more refreshed and rejuvenated look.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes by reducing inflammation caused by allergens, stress, or other irritants.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of English Lavender essential oil can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage, reducing the appearance of fine lines, wrinkles, and discoloration that can contribute to puffiness.

Calming and relaxing: The calming and relaxing effects of English Lavender essential oil can help reduce tension and stress, which are common causes of puffy eyes, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are often used to create a calming and soothing oil that can help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.
Buds: Lavender buds are a popular choice for making essential oils for puffy eyes, as they contain a high concentration of calming compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when they are fully bloomed and free of dew.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or use a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to crush the dried flowers into a fine powder and then transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of high-proof vodka to the jar and let it steep for 2 weeks, shaking the mixture daily, to extract the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids, leaving behind the clear lavandula angustifolia essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The gentle oil reduces swelling and puffiness, making it an effective natural solution for dark circles and bags under the eyes. It also possesses antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and contribute to puffiness. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects promote relaxation, reducing tension and promoting a sense of calm, further alleviating puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness by calming irritated skin and tissues around the eyes, alleviating conditions like conjunctivitis and blepharitis.

Antioxidant: Chamomile essential oil's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, protecting the delicate eye area from oxidative stress and damage.

Calming and Soothing: Chamomile essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin and tissues around the eyes, which helps reduce redness, itching, and irritation that can exacerbate puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes.
Flowers: Known for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective treatment for soothing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days.
Crush 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient steam distillation.
Steam distill the crushed flower mixture with 2 cups of water at 100°c (212°f) for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting oil from the condenser and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with puffy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation also helps to decongest and reduce fluid retention, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing puffiness. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving you with a refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and puffiness by reducing inflammation around the eyes, providing relief from water retention and allergies.
Antioxidant: As an antioxidant, rosemary essential oil helps to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.

Circulation-stimulating: Rosemary essential oil's stimulating properties help to increase blood flow and circulation around the eyes, which can reduce puffiness by allowing excess fluid to be drained away more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant are valued for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and soothing irritated eyes.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat eye infections and reduce inflammation, making them a valuable ingredient in essential oils for puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in large quantities.
Steam-distill the plant material using a suitable apparatus for at least 2 hours.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distillation process.
Filter and store the essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids.
Use high-quality testing equipment to verify the quality and purity of the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. When applied topically around the orbital bone, tea tree oil reduces swelling and redness, making it an effective remedy for puffiness caused by fatigue, allergies, or hormonal changes. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to purify the skin, further reducing puffiness and leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tea tree essential oil contains antioxidants that help reduce puffiness by neutralizing free radicals and inflammation that can cause swelling in the eye area.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree essential oil, such as terpinen-4-ol, can help reduce inflammation and ease congestion that contributes to puffy eyes.

Antibacterial properties: Tea tree essential oil's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that can cause puffiness and redness in the eye area, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree plant contain high amounts of cineole, a compound that helps reduce swelling and inflammation associated with puffy eyes.
Stems: Tea tree stems are rich in terpinen-4-ol, an antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that can help soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and dry them immediately to prevent degradation.
Combine 500g dried leaves with 1000ml distilled water in a large glass container and steep for 24 hours at room temperature.
Filter the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Use a distillation apparatus to separate the essential oil from the infused liquid over the course of 2-3 hours at low pressure.
Transfer the obtained melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and congestion. The oil's antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, sesame essential oil improves blood circulation, which helps to de-puff the area by removing excess fluid and leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sesame essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which can reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area by inhibiting inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Sesame essential oil is rich in antioxidants like vitamin E and linoleic acid, which can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from free radical damage and oxidative stress that can cause puffiness.

Natural vasodilator properties: The sesamol content in sesame essential oil can dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on the eyes and alleviating puffiness caused by fluid retention or sinus congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sesame roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Seeds: Sesame seeds contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum flowers in full bloom and dry them completely within 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Crush the dried sesamum indicum flowers into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Transfer the crushed sesamum indicum flowers to a clean glass jar and add 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, before filtering it through cheesecloth.
Store the resulting sesamum indicum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use it within 6 months or after opening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and swelling. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties work to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, wild geranium has a diuretic effect, which helps to alleviate excess fluid retention that can contribute to eye puffiness. When used topically in a gentle massage or applied with a cotton pad under the eyes, wild geranium essential oils provide fast relief from puffy, tired-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild Geranium essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, providing relief from eye strain and fatigue.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Wild Geranium essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to skin cells and contribute to puffy eyes, leaving the area looking smoother and more youthful.

Diuretic: The diuretic properties of Wild Geranium essential oil can help reduce fluid retention, which is a common cause of puffy eyes, by increasing urine production and reducing water buildup under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild geranium plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling, making them effective in alleviating puffiness and discomfort around the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the wild geranium plant are rich in tannins and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eye areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried geranium maculatum leaves at dawn when they are most aromatic.
Combine one cup of harvested geranium maculatum leaves with three cups of water in a large glass jar.
Steam the mixture for 30 minutes using a double boiler setup, ensuring optimal oil extraction.
Strain the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding solids.
Allow the extracted oil to mature for seven days at room temperature before use or storage in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and soothe irritation. The oil's flavonoids and limonene compounds also help to constrict blood vessels, decreasing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, bitter orange essential oil has natural astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to tighten and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and discoloration, leaving your eyes looking brighter and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bergamot Essential Oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Bergamot Essential Oil can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye fatigue, dark circles, and puffiness.
Decongestant: Bergamot Essential Oil's decongestant properties can help reduce fluid retention in the eyes and alleviate puffy eyes caused by allergies or sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant are used to create a calming and soothing oil that helps to reduce inflammation and puffiness in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the bitter orange plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus peel from organic citrus x aurantium trees weighing about 500g.
Dry the harvested citrus peels in a clean well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Transfer dried citrus peels to a mason jar and add 250ml of high-proof vodka for maceration.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days while occasionally shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal essential oils is to gently massage a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and lavender oils into the orbital bone using a circular motion. Peppermint oil helps constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness, while chamomile and lavender soothe and calm the skin. Repeat as needed to reduce swelling and leave eyes looking refreshed.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal essential oils are dark circles, eye fatigue, and dry eyes. Lavender oil can reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes, while peppermint oil can cool and refresh the eyes. Geranium oil can help alleviate dark circles by improving circulation and reducing water retention. These herbal essential oils can be used topically or in a warm compress to provide relief from these common eye issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, the oil's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of puffiness. As a result, Aloe barbadensis essential oils promote a more refreshed and revitalized look, making it an effective natural remedy for puffy eyes. Regular use can lead to a more radiant and youthful appearance.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Cinnamaldehyde: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate puffiness and reduce oxidative stress around the eyes.
Furofuran lignans: Possesses anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, which help reduce blood flow to the affected area and alleviate puffiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves is widely used due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Barks: The bark of Aloe barbadensis is used to create essential oils that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe barbadensis leaves with a sharp knife to prevent damage to the plant.
Extract gel from the leaves using a sharp knife or a juicer within 5 minutes.
Distill the gel in a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Collect the distillate in a condenser and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any remaining impurities within 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cucumis sativus essential oils helps with puffy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease congestion. The oil's cooling and soothing effects calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
Cucurbitacins: As flavonoids, cucurbitacins possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate puffiness and redness in the eyes.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and promote healing around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease eye puffiness.
Flowers: Cucumber flowers contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can soothe and calm puffy eyes.
Seeds: Cucumber seeds contain oil that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye puffiness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3 kg of fresh cucumis sativus flowers at dawn for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a 300-liter still for 6 hours to extract oil.
Collect 1.5 liters of extracted oil and filter it through a 5-micron filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in dark glass bottles at 4 degrees celsius for 2 months to mature.
Use a gc-ms analyzer to verify the oil's chemical composition and ensure it meets quality standards.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil, extracted from the Calendula flower, contains triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. By soothing and calming the delicate skin around the eyes, Calendula essential oil reduces swelling and puffiness, making it an effective natural remedy for tired, puffy eyes. Its gentle and non-irritating nature makes it suitable for sensitive skin.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with puffy eyes.

Carotenoids (including Beta-Carotene): As antioxidants, carotenoids help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing puffiness and promoting a more even appearance.
Phenolic Acids (including Ursolic Acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Calendula flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Leaves: Calendula leaves are also used to make essential oils that help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Buds: Calendula buds are used to make essential oils that help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of calendula officinalis flowers during the peak season with morning dew still present on petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer for 7 days at 35 degrees celsius temperature.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 1:5 flower to water ratio for 3 hours at 100 degrees celsius pressure.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at 5 degrees celsius temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana essential oils helps with puffy eyes because its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties reduce swelling and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce fluid retention also helps to minimize puffiness. remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This tannin helps reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and improving lymphatic drainage, thus alleviating water retention and swelling.
Ferulic acid: As a phenolic compound, ferulic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness by minimizing swelling and redness associated with eye strain and irritation.
Triterpenoids: These compounds, including hamamelol and hamamelol-2, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing puffiness and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Buds: The buds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm and protect the delicate skin around the eyes.
Barks: The barks are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature plants at dawn or midday to capture optimal essential oil content.
Steam distill 100g of leaves in 500ml of water at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract oil.
Separate oil from water by decantation or centrifugation and collect the essential oil.
Filter the oil through a 5µm filter to remove impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the oil in dark glass bottles at 10-20°c to preserve its quality and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: A polyphenol that helps reduce inflammation and edema, which contribute to puffy eyes.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used to make essential oils due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.

Buds: Vitis vinifera buds are used to make essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce puffiness and tighten the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the ripe vitis vinifera fruits in early fall when they have reached their full color and have been properly dried for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried vitis vinifera fruits for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil from their cells.
Collect the obtained essential oil from the distillation apparatus and transfer it to a clean glass container for storage.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity and appearance.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia trigona essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Flavonoids: They possess potent antioxidant properties that help reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffiness in the eyes.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil from Euphorbia trigona leaves is used due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes.
Stems: Euphorbia trigona stem essential oil is used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Buds: Euphorbia trigona bud essential oil is used due to its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and promote relaxation in the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphorbia trigona leaves and stems at dawn when the plant is fully charged with morning dew and sunlight.
Dry the harvested leaves and stems in a warm dry place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a stainless steel distiller at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the steam and discard the solids and water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Licoricidin: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing the puffiness and redness of the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.
Glycyrrhizin: Possesses anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help to reduce water retention and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra to make essential oils for puffy eyes because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to calm and soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are used in essential oils for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce puffiness and promote a healthy, radiant appearance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the roots in a 100 kg steam distillation apparatus for 4 hours at 100°c temperature.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a 50 kg separator vessel for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper for 15 minutes to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in a 1 liter amber glass bottle at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The oil's antioxidant properties also help combat free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness. Furthermore, Echinacea purpurea is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to improve circulation and reduce fluid retention. This natural combination of properties makes Echinacea purpurea essential oil an effective remedy for alleviating puffy eyes and promoting a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_constituents: Isobutyl amides: These amides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate puffy eyes by minimizing the body's inflammatory response.

Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to puffy eyes, while also promoting overall eye health.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating puffy eyes and promoting healthy eye function.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Roots: The roots are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and promote overall eye health.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote circulation, which can help alleviate puffy eyes and improve overall eye well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning when dew is still present and the sun is low.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags or a clean cloth in a warm area for 2 weeks.
Use a commercial steam distillation unit to extract the essential oil from the dried flowers for 8 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water using a separatory funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a cotton cloth to remove any impurities and stabilize the oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation also helps to reduce the appearance of dark circles and puffiness by providing essential oxygen and nutrients to the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, its antioxidant properties protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a smoother and more radiant complexion. As a result, Ginkgo biloba essential oil is a natural and effective solution for combating puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce puffiness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to dilate and contribute to swelling.
Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness.
Bilobalide: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye puffiness.
Seeds: Contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce swelling and promote relaxation, making them useful for treating puffy eyes.
Barks: Used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate puffiness and discomfort around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves at 100°f using a 1:5 leaf-to-water ratio for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a dropper top at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and promote relaxation. The oil's ability to soothe and calm the skin also helps to alleviate puffiness. Additionally, it improves circulation, allowing excess fluids to be drained, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes. The oil's natural astringent properties also help to tighten the skin, giving the area a smoother and more refreshed appearance. This makes it a popular remedy for tired and puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes.
Naphthodianthrones: These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm puffy eyes.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in puffy eyes.
Flowers: Employed for their soothing and calming effects to reduce puffiness and promote relaxation around the eye area.
Stems: Utilized for their astringent properties to help reduce water retention and alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers at the peak of blooming in full sun conditions.
Steam distill 100 grams of the fresh flowers for 3 hours in a copper still.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.
